highlights.summary: Unifies and refactors Trio's alerting pipeline: introduces a single TrioAlertManager that routes LoopKit device and glucose alerts, adds a configurable Glucose Alarms list (including new "Carbs Required"), tiered device/pump alarm severity with per-tier snooze and shared Day/Night window, catalog-based pump alert classification, unified in-app banner + UserNotification scheduling and centralized snooze handling. Moves the global Snooze UI from a hidden long-press into Settings → Notifications (canonical 15/60/180/360‑minute durations), deletes the old Snooze module and legacy SwiftMessages pipeline, and migrates callers to TrioAlertManager. Adds bundled CAF tones and a CriticalAlertAudioPlayer fallback, includes the time-sensitive UN entitlement plumbing, and replaces the Not‑Looping monitor with a re-armed delayed LoopKit alert. Accompanying unit tests, settings/UI changes, and fixes to snooze UI gesture handling were merged.
highlights.userFacingImpact: Alerts are now routed through a unified alert manager and Settings gains new sections (Glucose Alarms; Device/Pump & CGM Alarms; Day & Night Window). Glucose alarms become a configurable list (with per-alarm thresholds, schedule, sound and snooze) and a new "Carbs Required" alarm type is added. Device/pump alerts are configured by severity tier with per-tier sound/Override‑Silence behavior. The global Snooze All control is visible in Settings → Notifications (replaces the hidden long-press), using 15/60/180/360‑minute options. If a CGM app owns glucose alerts, Trio surfaces those under “Handled by CGM App” and suppresses duplicate alerts. In-app banner vs background UN behavior is unified; snooze actions are centralized so quick-snooze actions behave consistently. The Not‑Looping alert now uses a delayed re-arm and legacy SwiftMessages alerting and its dependency were removed. Minor test and UI fixes for snooze long-press behavior were included.

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1203: Refactor Trio Alert/Notification Handling – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [1/3]
nightscout/Trio#1203: Refactor Trio Alert/Notification Handling – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [1/3]

Introduce new alert system to Trio * Route LoopKit alerts through TrioAlertManager * BaseDeviceDataManager.issueAlert/retractAlert forward to new TrioAlertManager * Drop the AlertObserver auto-ack and pumpNotification broadcast * TrioAlertCategory enum + classifier covers Omni, Dana, Minimed     alert identifiers; .commsTransient and .algorithmError dwell-     suppressed (count=2 or 60s), everything else surfaces * APSManager.processError synthesises a LoopKit alert via the     classifier; manual bolus + cancelBolus catches emit explicit     .bolusFailed alerts so they always fire * SwiftUI in-app banner (iOS-push styling, SF Symbol on tinted     square, live timestamp, tap/swipe-to-ack, stacked deck with     critical-on-top, tap top to expand) * Drop notificationsPump/Cgm/Carb/Algorithm settings * Test alert buttons in PumpConfig (need to be removed later)
Alerts: critical-audio fallback, snooze unification, forecasted-low
Glucose Alarms: user-configurable list of alarms
Device Alarms: tiered config + force-loop dwell bypass
Replace substring alert classifier with catalog-based routing
Add time-sensitive UN entitlement so alerts pierce Focus modes
Alarm windows, per-variant toggle, not-looping pipeline, UN cleanup
Snooze: unify across pipeline + in-app banner affordances
Route carbs-required through TrioAlertManager + retract when resolved
Cover xDrip4iOS in CGM-owns-alerts gate; expose owning app name and deep link
Surface CGM-owned alarms in their own section with a linked app name

highlights.summary: Adds Quick-Pick Boluses: long-press the home + button to open a suggestions sheet populated from the last 90 days of manual bolus history scored by a LoopFollow-derived algorithm (time-of-day, weekday/weekend weighting, recency). Up to five suggested amounts are shown, deduplicated/filtered by minimum score. Selecting a suggestion requires biometric authentication and a slide-to-enact confirmation; the feature includes a settings toggle (Enable Quick Bolus), help sheet, localization/formatter updates, and UI/safety guards.
highlights.userFacingImpact: New Quick-Pick Boluses are available via long-pressing the + button on the home screen; suggestions are derived from your manual bolus history (or a "no history" explanation if insufficient data). Enacting a suggestion requires on-device authentication and explicit confirmation; the feature can be disabled via the new "Enable Quick Bolus" setting (persisted and included in settings export).

highlights.summary: Switches the default Oref implementation to the Swift algorithm: the persisted setting was renamed from useSwiftOref to useJavascriptOref (default false), runtime branching inverted so Swift is used when useJavascriptOref is false, and UI/labels were updated to present "Use JavaScript Oref" as the opt-in legacy toggle. Call sites and function signatures in OpenAPS/APSManager were updated accordingly and LoopStatusView tag logic was inverted.
highlights.userFacingImpact: The app now defaults to the Swift Oref algorithm; users who need the legacy JavaScript implementation must enable the new "Use JavaScript Oref" toggle. On-screen hints and labels were updated to explain that Swift is the default and JavaScript is the legacy option.

items.summary: Updates localization resource Trio/Sources/Localizations/Main/Localizable.xcstrings from Crowdin (2026-07-03): multiple translations updated (notably Norwegian nb-NO replacing “blodsukker” with “glukose”), new localization keys/entries added (punctuation symbols, device-alert label, relative time strings), and several snooze-duration entries and comments were altered.
items.userFacingImpact: Users will see updated UI text and translations (for example Norwegian wording changes from “blodsukker” to “glukose”) and new/adjusted localized labels such as device-alert text and relative time strings. Because some changes touch alarm-related strings, a human review of safety-critical phrasing is recommended.

items.summary: Simplifies GlucoseChartView point rendering by replacing a custom Image-based symbol closure with Swift Charts' built-in .symbol(.circle), extracting pointColor(for:) helper and preserving a custom Image(symbol) for manual readings. The PR notes no visual change but reduces per-mark view hierarchy overhead.
items.userFacingImpact: Per the PR description, visual appearance is unchanged. The change may reduce per-mark SwiftUI view-hierarchy overhead and could improve rendering performance; human review/benchmarking is recommended to confirm any performance impact.

sources.title: gordolio/Trio#1274: Replace Image in GlucoseChartView with symbol
nightscout/Trio#1274: Replace Image in GlucoseChartView with symbol

items.summary: CI/dev metadata bumps: multiple commits increment APP_DEV_VERSION in Config.xcconfig across development commits (0.8.4.1 → 0.8.4.2 → 0.8.4.3 → 0.8.4.4 → 0.8.4.5 → 0.8.4.6 as applicable).
items.userFacingImpact: These commits update development/build metadata (APP_DEV_VERSION) and do not show functional source changes in the provided diffs. They primarily affect dev/CI versioning and build metadata; if your release or CI relies on these values in other ways, validate those pipelines.

items.summary: Fastlane / provisioning capability and Fastfile refactors: Fastfile updated to include USERNOTIFICATIONS_TIMESENSITIVE capability handling; capability creation/update logic was refactored (switching between create_capability and update_capability approaches across commits), added verification and conditional team-id setting logic, and adjusted error behavior when capabilities remain missing.
items.userFacingImpact: These changes affect build/provisioning automation (Fastlane) and how App Store Connect bundle ID capabilities are created/updated during CI runs. Behaviour changes may cause different Fastlane outcomes (e.g., lane failures if capabilities remain missing or different creation/update flows). Review CI/automation that configures bundle IDs and capability management before relying on automated runs.

refactor(fastlane/Fastfile): Refactor capability update logic in Fastfile
refactor(fastlane): set current team ID for provisioning request client
refactor(fastlane): Refactor capability update logic in Fastfile

includedPullRequests.title: Refactor Trio Alert/Notification Handling – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [1/3]
includedPullRequests.body: ## Summary Refactors Trio's entire alert / notification handling. Routes all device + glucose alerts through a single `TrioAlertManager` (serves as LoopKit `AlertIssuer`/`AlertResponder`), introduces a user-configurable glucose alarm list (LoopFollow-style), tiered device-alarm list, a shared Day/Night window, and unifies snooze handling across phone / watch / banner / lock-screen quick-action. **Basic functionality:** If user has Trio in the foreground, we display an in-app banner. If Trio is backgrounded, we push a UserNotification. ## Changes - Adds glucose alarms via user-defined list (urgent-low / low / high / forecasted-low / carbs-required), each with own threshold, schedule, sound, per-window enable, snooze. Hierarchical suppression (urgent-low silences low; low family silences forecasted-low). - Adds `CGMManagerAlertOwnership` gate: when the active CGM provides its own glucose alerts (Dexcom G5 / G6 / One / G7 / One+, plus xDrip4iOS via the `.xdrip` App Group source), reading-driven alarms move into a dedicated "Handled by CGM App" section and the coordinator suppresses them. The "Use CGM App Alerts" toggle is auto-off + disabled when the active CGM has no companion app (e.g. LibreLoop), so users see clearly that Trio owns the alerting. The footer names the responsible app and renders the name as a deep link when a URL scheme is known. - Adds device alarms with three severity tiers (Critical / Time-Sensitive / Normal). Each tier holds multiple configs keyed by Day/Night/Always; pump alerts pick the matching variant. Snooze is per-tier. - Adds `AlertCatalogVendor` protocol + `AlertCatalogRegistry` — a per-pump catalog (Omnipod / Minimed / Dana / Medtrum, incl. Omnipod's dynamic `Fault Event Code 0xNN: …` hex parser) that maps each plugin-emitted `Alert.Identifier` to its `(concept, interruptionLevel, title, category)`. Replaces the previous substring classifier; concept-based dedupe collapses cross-plugin duplicates ("Reservoir Empty" etc.) in the Settings UI. - CGM lifecycle alerts (sensor failure / expiry / signal loss) bypass the catalog and pass through with the plugin's chosen interruption level. - Adds configurable Day & Night Window that is shared across alarms; default day starts 06:00, night starts 22:00 (or the AM/PM equivalent). - Adds sound library of 17 bundled `.caf` tones; per-alarm picker with audible preview, override-Silence-&-Focus toggle. Ported from Loop's `next-dev` branch (thanks @ps2). Alarm row surfaces sound on/off and Overrides-Focus as separate inline tags so "silent but haptic-pierces-Focus" is visible. - Adds critical-audio fallback: `CriticalAlertAudioPlayer` plays via `AVAudioSession(.playback)` + `MPVolumeView` for builds without the Critical Alerts entitlement; iOS otherwise silently downgrades and DnD / silent switch mute the sound. Partially taken from PR #392, inspired by both LoopFollow (thanks @bjorkert) and Loop's `next-dev` (thanks @ps2). - Adds `com.apple.developer.usernotifications.time-sensitive` entitlement so `.timeSensitive` alerts pierce Focus modes. - Replaces the legacy "Not-Looping" monitor with a re-armed delayed alert: every loop success cancels and re-schedules a `delayed(20m)` alert via LoopKit's `Alert.Trigger`. Background-survivable; retracts cleanly when loop and/or app recovers. Skipped automatically when closed loop is off, a manual temp basal is active, or the pump is suspended — those states imply the user isn't expecting auto-enactment. - Adds **Carbs Required** as a configurable `GlucoseAlert` type alongside the existing low/high/forecasted family. Configured under `Settings → Glucose Alarms → Carbs Required` with its own threshold (grams), schedule, sound, snooze, and Override Silence & Focus toggle. The alarm threshold is **independent** from the home-screen tab badge's threshold (which lives in `Settings → Features → UI → Carbs Required Threshold`), so users can have a low-threshold passive badge and a higher-threshold active interrupt. Existing installs get the new alarm backfilled on launch alongside any other types added by later releases. - Add-Alarm picker enforces a one-alarm-per-window constraint per type. Types whose Day & Night coverage is fully occupied (either a single `.always` alarm or both `.day` + `.night` set) show up under an `Unavailable` section in the picker with a footer hint pointing at the existing alarm. The editor's "Active During" dropdown is filtered to options that wouldn't collide. - Adds new in-app banner in iOS-push style; stacked deck with critical-always-on-top, tap or swipe up to snooze 15min by default, long-press for context-menu to snooze (15m / 1h / 3h / 6h). ## Test coverage - Per-pump alert-emission + classification: `Omnipod`, `Minimed`, `Dana`, `Medtrum`, `Tandem`, `RileyLink`. - Catalog: `AlertCatalogRegistryEntriesTests`, `AlertCatalogRegistryOmniFaultTests` (hex-prefix parser). - Glucose alarms: `GlucoseAlertTests`, `GlucoseAlertCoordinatorTests` (incl. carbs-required predicate guards), `GlucoseAlertConfigurationTests` (incl. DST spring-forward / fall-back), `ForecastedGlucoseEvaluatorTests`, `GlucoseAlertsStoreTests` (seed + backfill + Add-picker window gating). - Ownership lookup: `CGMManagerAlertOwnershipTests` (manager + `.xdrip` source paths + deep-link mapping). - Plumbing: `AlertMuterTests`, `DeviceAlertsStoreTests` (variant precedence + per-tier snooze), `TrioAlertCategoryTests`, `TrioModalAlertSchedulerTests`, `NotLoopingMonitorTests`. ## Screenshots | Critical Alert | Time-Sensitive Alert | Info Alert | Snoozing | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Day & Night Windows | Glucose Alarms | Device Alarms | Sound Config (w/ Play) | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Device Alarm Form | Device Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Addition | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | ## Additional notes - Old per-category settings are gone. Alarms now live in the new modules (`Settings → Glucose Alarms / Device Alarms / Day & Night Window`). - Pump alerts no longer auto-acknowledge; they surface through the unified in-app banner + UserNotification pipeline until the user (or the device) clears them. - Critical interruption level pierces snooze; Time-Sensitive / Active are fully suppressed during a snooze window.

Types whose Day & Night coverage is fully occupied (either a single `.always` alarm or both `.day` + `.night` set) show up under an `Unavailable` section in the picker with a footer hint pointing at the existing alarm. The editor's "Active During" dropdown is filtered to options that wouldn't collide. - Adds new in-app banner in iOS-push style; stacked deck with critical-always-on-top, tap or swipe up to snooze 15min by default, long-press for context-menu to snooze (15m / 1h / 3h / 6h). ## Test coverage - Per-pump alert-emission + classification: `Omnipod`, `Minimed`, `Dana`, `Medtrum`, `Tandem`, `RileyLink`. - Catalog: `AlertCatalogRegistryEntriesTests`, `AlertCatalogRegistryOmniFaultTests` (hex-prefix parser). - Glucose alarms: `GlucoseAlertTests`, `GlucoseAlertCoordinatorTests` (incl. carbs-required predicate guards), `GlucoseAlertConfigurationTests` (incl. DST spring-forward / fall-back), `ForecastedGlucoseEvaluatorTests`, `GlucoseAlertsStoreTests` (seed + backfill + Add-picker window gating). - Ownership lookup: `CGMManagerAlertOwnershipTests` (manager + `.xdrip` source paths + deep-link mapping). - Plumbing: `AlertMuterTests`, `DeviceAlertsStoreTests` (variant precedence + per-tier snooze), `TrioAlertCategoryTests`, `TrioModalAlertSchedulerTests`, `NotLoopingMonitorTests`. ## Screenshots | Critical Alert | Time-Sensitive Alert | Info Alert | Snoozing | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Day & Night Windows | Glucose Alarms | Device Alarms | Sound Config (w/ Play) | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | | Device Alarm Form | Device Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Form | Glucose Alarm Addition | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | ## Additional notes - Old per-category settings are gone. Alarms now live in the new modules (`Settings → Glucose Alarms / Device Alarms / Day & Night Window`). - Pump alerts no longer auto-acknowledge; they surface through the unified in-app banner + UserNotification pipeline until the user (or the device) clears them. - Critical interruption level pierces snooze; Time-Sensitive / Active are fully suppressed during a snooze window.

includedPullRequests.title: Move "Snooze All" to Settings and Clean up Legacy Alerting; Follow-Up to #1203 – Prepare O5 and Libre 3/3+ Integration [3/3]
includedPullRequests.body:
NEEDS TO BE MERGED AFTER #1203 LANDS IN `feat/o5` BRANCH.

## Summary

- **Snooze All** moves from a hidden long-press on the home glucose bobble to a prominent button in **Settings → Notifications**. Tapping it opens an inline sheet with the four canonical durations (15 min / 1 hr / 3 hrs / 6 hrs), each calling `TrioAlertManager.applySnooze(for:)` directly.
- **Snooze module deleted.** No more module / router round-trip, no obsolete `GlucoseAlarm` enum, no stray 20 min option.
- **Legacy SwiftMessages alert pipeline removed.** `Router.alertMessage`, `MessageContent`, `MessageType`, `MessageSubtype`, `NotificationAction`, `alertMessageNotificationObserver`, `MainStateModel`'s SwiftMessages dispatch, and the dead `.pumpConfig` UN-tap deep-link are all gone. `SwiftMessages` SwiftPM dependency dropped.
- **4 real callers migrated** to `TrioAlertManager`:
  - Calibration: "Glucose is stale for calibration"
  - GarminManager: "Garmin Connect must be installed"
  - CGM Settings (×2): "Unable to open the app" / "No URL available"
- **`Logger.info()`** is now log-only — matches `warning` / `debug`. Banner side-effect removed.
- **`TimeInterval` convenience helpers** (`init(hours:)`, `init(minutes:)`, getters, statics) extracted from the deleted Snooze module into `Trio/Sources/Helpers/TimeInterval+Convenience.swift`, since callers outside the alert pipeline rely on them.

Net effect: every user-facing alert in the app flows through `TrioAlertManager`. No more parallel SwiftMessages path.

Follow-up to PR #1203.

includedPullRequests.title: Add a Quick Bolus feature
includedPullRequests.body: Inspired by @bjorkert 's work on Loop Follow, I have added a Quick Bolus feature. **Why?** Users who pre-bolus and/or do not register carbs still have to go through the Treatments sheet, tap the bolus field, add amount, and confirm. When you "just want to bolus", the Treatments sheet is a bit complex. **How does it work?** Tap and hold the + button to open the Quick Bolus feature. It will look at your bolus history for the last 90 days and suggest up to 5 different amounts that you usually give at the same time-of-day. It will distinguish between weekdays and weekends, and give less weight to older bolus data. **Screenshots** If you have no bolus history, it will let you know: Bolus history of 1U and 2U: Quick Bolus suggests 1U and 2U: More historic values: More suggestions: After selecting a value, you are asked to confirm. Biometric control is applied after confirmation. Information/help sheet:

Biometric control is applied after confirmation. Information/help sheet:

includedPullRequests.title: Use Swift Oref by default
includedPullRequests.body:
This change updates the oref swift setting to be an oref javascript
setting instead, effectively defaulting all users into the Swift
implementation.

By defaulting people into the Swift implementation we will get a
chance to find any lingering issues with the implementation before
removing the Javascript code from the codebase. If there are any
issues, people can switch back to JS without needing to recompile.

includedPullRequests.title: Use Swift Oref by default
includedPullRequests.body:
This change updates the oref swift setting to be an oref javascript
setting instead, effectively defaulting all users into the Swift
implementation.

By defaulting people into the Swift implementation we will get a
chance to find any lingering issues with the implementation before
removing the Javascript code from the codebase. If there are any
issues, people can switch back to JS without needing to recompile.

includedPullRequests.title: Replace Image in GlucoseChartView with symbol
includedPullRequests.body: CGM readings now use Swift Charts' built-in `.symbol(.circle)` instead of a custom `.symbol { Image(...) } `closure. Custom symbol views make Charts host a full SwiftUI view hierarchy per mark. Visually nothing has changed.

includedCommits.subject: Introduce new alert system to Trio * Route LoopKit alerts through TrioAlertManager * BaseDeviceDataManager.issueAlert/retractAlert forward to new TrioAlertManager * Drop the AlertObserver auto-ack and pumpNotification broadcast * TrioAlertCategory enum + classifier covers Omni, Dana, Minimed     alert identifiers; .commsTransient and .algorithmError dwell-     suppressed (count=2 or 60s), everything else surfaces * APSManager.processError synthesises a LoopKit alert via the     classifier; manual bolus + cancelBolus catches emit explicit     .bolusFailed alerts so they always fire * SwiftUI in-app banner (iOS-push styling, SF Symbol on tinted     square, live timestamp, tap/swipe-to-ack, stacked deck with     critical-on-top, tap top to expand) * Drop notificationsPump/Cgm/Carb/Algorithm settings * Test alert buttons in PumpConfig (need to be removed later)
